channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: More Bungie Layoffs Announced Including Most of The Destiny Team
item.content:encoded:
It's a day that ends in y in the video game industry, so as seems inevitable, there is an announcement of layoffs, this time from Sony Interactive Entertainment and primarily their studio Bungie.

The news from from both Bungie, and Herman Hulst, the CEO of Studio Business Group of Sony Interactive Entertainment. Hulst states the layoffs are reduction in workforce that affect "a significant number of employees, including most of the Destiny team and some Marathon team members. There are also reductions across SIE teams that support Bungie’s operations. Those impacted at Bungie and within SIE are being informed today."

He goes onto explain that Marathon 'remains an important part of our portfolio', and they will 'continue to support the team as they build on the strong foundation established in season 1 and 2'. He is also 'encouraged' by the creativity and opportunities in various incubation projects that Bungie has ongoing.

Bungie's own post on the matter opens with them expressing 'great sadness' that they are 'announcing a reduction in force' as they reorganize. It goes onto state:

"As the leaders of Bungie, past and present, we recognize Destiny 2 fell short of expectations these past several years. Following our final content update to Destiny 2, and with our future projects still in early incubation, we could not continue operating at our previous size."

As always seems to be the case, it will be less those leaders paying for the mistakes they admit to making, and more on many of the rank and file members who created the games, something that no amount of calling it difficult, or expressing sadness can cover up. One only hopes that where Sony says they are providing support that will involve a significant remuneration package, especially given that being based in the United States, Bungie has less labor protections than studios set in different countries.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
